quests_completed: SQL Raid: Restructure Indian Govt CPI Data

daily_quests.title: ASX 200 Momentum Analysis with Window Functions
daily_quests.description: Using the ASX 200 historical prices dataset, write a SQL query with window functions to calculate a 20-day moving average of closing prices and rank stocks by their price momentum (percentage change from 20 days ago). Include columns: ticker, date, close_price, moving_avg_20day, price_change_pct, and momentum_rank (ranked within each stock, ordered by date). Filter for the top 10 most volatile stocks (highest standard deviation of daily returns) in the last 90 days. Use CTEs to structure the query logically: first calculate daily returns, then moving averages, then rank by momentum. Expected output: 900+ rows showing daily momentum rankings for top 10 stocks.

daily_quests.title: NSW Road Crash Data Cleaning & Hotspot Mapping
daily_quests.description: Download the NSW Road Crash Data (CSV format) and perform a comprehensive data cleaning pipeline in Python/pandas. Tasks: (1) Handle missing values in crash_type, severity, and location columns using appropriate strategies (drop/forward-fill/mode imputation); (2) Standardise postcode values to 4-digit format; (3) Remove duplicate crash records based on crash_id and timestamp; (4) Create new features: hour_of_day (from datetime), day_of_week, is_fatal (boolean from severity); (5) Identify and flag outliers in latitude/longitude using IQR method; (6) Export cleaned dataset to CSV with a summary report showing: rows removed, null percentages before/after, and top 5 crash hotspot postcodes by frequency. Expected output: cleaned CSV file + summary text file.

daily_quests.title: Australian Weather Data ETL: Temperature Anomalies Detection
daily_quests.description: Build an end-to-end ETL pipeline combining Python and SQL. Part 1 (Python): Download Australian Bureau of Meteorology weather observations (daily max/min temps, rainfall) for 3 major cities (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane) from the last 5 years. Clean the data using pandas: standardise date format, handle missing rainfall values with 0, remove temperature readings outside -10°C to 50°C range. Create a feature: temp_anomaly_flag (TRUE if max_temp is >2 std devs above the city's historical mean for that month). Export to CSV. Part 2 (SQL): Load cleaned CSV into a database table called weather_observations. Write a SQL query using window functions (LAG, ROW_NUMBER, NTILE) to: (1) calculate 7-day rolling average temperature; (2) identify consecutive days of anomalies per city; (3) rank anomaly events by severity (days_in_sequence * temp_deviation). Output: list of top 10 anomaly events with city, start_date, duration_days, avg_deviation. Expected deliverables: cleaned CSV + SQL script + results table showing anomaly events.
daily_quests.dataset: Australian Weather Observations — Bureau of Meteorology / Kaggle (jsphyg)
